perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential Information” means any and all information (including , without limitation, all business, financial, commerci al and technical information and data) , in whatever form transmitted, whether tangible or intangible, that is disclosed, furnished or otherwise provided or made available by the d isclosing p arty to the receiving p arty prior to or after the date of this Agreement .
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential Information” means only tangible documents, materials, and data clearly marked as confidential at the time of disclosure. This explicitly excludes oral communications and any information not physically labeled as such.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines "Confidential Information" broadly. The changed text restricts it to only tangible items marked confidential. This creates a contradiction in the definition itself, as the original definition includes both tangible and intangible information, while the modified version limits it to only tangible and labeled items. This contradicts section 2 where obligations are extended to any Confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the Tennessee Public Records Act, Section 10 -7-5-1 et seq., a t all times during the term of this Agreement and thereafter, the receiving party shall: (i) refrain from disclosing , directly or indirectly, any Confidential Information of the disclosing party to third parties , and hold all Confidential Information received from the disclosing party in trust and strict confidence, subject to the terms of this Agreement ; (ii) take reasonable security precautions, at least as great as the precautions it takes to protect its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care, to keep confidential the disclosing party’s Confidential Information and avoid d isclosure, loss or misuse of the Confidential Information; and (iii) refrain from using any of the disclosing party’s Confidential Information for any purpose not specified her eunder (including , without limitation , in its own business or for its own benefit) without the prior written consent of the disclosing p arty.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Confidential Information' broadly, encompassing tangible and intangible information. The changed text narrows the definition to only tangible documents marked as confidential. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which imposes obligations on the receiving party regarding 'any Confidential Information'. By limiting the definition, the changed text implies that the obligations in the contradicted text only apply to explicitly marked tangible documents, whereas the original definition applied to a much broader set of information, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Any trade secrets will be entitled to all of the protections and benefits of applicable trade secret laws and any other applicable laws. For avoidance of doubt, the parties acknowledge and agree that, in the event a court of competent jurisdiction determines that information the disclosing p arty deems to be a trade secret is not a trade secret under applicable law, such information will nevertheless be deemed to be Confidential Information for purposes hereof, provided such information falls within the description set forth in Section 1(a).
perturbation.changed_text: Any trade secrets will be entitled to none of the protections and benefits of applicable trade secret laws and any other applicable laws. For avoidance of doubt, the parties acknowledge and agree that, in the event a court of competent jurisdiction determines that information the disclosing party deems to be a trade secret is not a trade secret under applicable law, such information will nevertheless be deemed to be Confidential Information for purposes hereof, provided such information falls within the description set forth in Section 1(a).
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that trade secrets are entitled to the protections of trade secret laws. The modified text reverses this, denying trade secrets those protections. This contradicts the assumed legal protection of trade secrets, thus resulting in an internal contradiction. This change directly contradicts the first sentence of the original text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any trade secrets will be entitled to none of the protections and benefits of applicable trade secret laws and any other applicable laws. For avoidance of doubt, the parties acknowledge and agree that, in the event a court of competent jurisdiction determines that information the disclosing p arty deems to be a trade secret is not a trade secret under applicable law, such information will nevertheless be deemed to be Confidential Information for purposes hereof, provided such information falls within the description set forth in Section 1(a).
perturbation.justification: The original text states that trade secrets *will* be entitled to protections, while the changed text states that trade secrets *will not* be entitled to protections. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text. Therefore the change creates a direct and strong in-text contradiction, satisfying the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, “Confidential Information” shall not mean information that, as demonstrate d by the receiving party , is (i) in the public domain prior to the date of this Agreement through no wrongful act of the receiving party or its agents or employees, (ii) independently developed by the receiving party without any use of, or reference to, Confidential Information, (iii) rightfully furnished to the receiving party by any third party without violation or breach of any confidentiality restriction, or (iv) authorized in writing for release by the disclosing party .
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, “Confidential Information” shall not mean information that, as demonstrated by the receiving party, is (i) in the public domain prior to the date of this Agreement regardless of the receiving party's actions or inactions, (ii) deemed publicly available regardless of its actual availability, (iii) rightfully furnished to the receiving party by any third party, or (iv) authorized orally for release by the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies conditions under which information is *not* considered Confidential Information, including stipulations against wrongful acts, independent development and written authorization. The change makes 'in the public domain' irrespective of the receiving party's actions, implies a 'deemed publicly available' concept regardless of reality, and allows 'oral authorization' for release which removes previous formal requirements. This undermines the core obligations in Section 2 regarding protecting Confidential Information and the written form of authorization as it states that the Confidential Information can be released verbally.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Subject to the Tennessee Public Records Act, Section 10 -7-5-1 et seq.,w ithout the written consent of the disclos ing party, the receiving party will not disclose the disclosing party’s Confidential Information to any person other than the receiving party’s officers, directors, owners, employees, agents and representatives (i) with a valid need to know such Confidenti al Information and (ii) that have an enforceable agreement with the receiving party containing confidentiality obligations substantially similar to those terms and conditions applicable to the receiving party under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the exceptions to what constitutes 'Confidential Information' by allowing information to be considered public domain regardless of the receiving party's actions and by permitting oral authorization for release. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which mandates written consent for disclosing Confidential Information, creating a clear and direct conflict. The original text specifically had stipulations against wrongful acts, independent development and written authorization, which is now eliminated.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to the Tennessee Public Records Act, Section 10 -7-5-1 et seq., a t all times during the term of this Agreement and thereafter, the receiving party shall: (i) refrain from disclosing , directly or indirectly, any Confidential Information of the disclosing party to third parties , and hold all Confidential Information received from the disclosing party in trust and strict confidence, subject to the terms of this Agreement ; (ii) take reasonable security precautions, at least as great as the precautions it takes to protect its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care, to keep confidential the disclosing party’s Confidential Information and avoid d isclosure, loss or misuse of the Confidential Information; and (iii) refrain from using any of the disclosing party’s Confidential Information for any purpose not specified her eunder (including , without limitation , in its own business or for its own benefit) without the prior written consent of the disclosing p arty.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the Tennessee Public Records Act, Section 10 -7-5-1 et seq., a t all times during the term of this Agreement and thereafter, the receiving party may disclose, directly or indirectly, any Confidential Information of the disclosing party to third parties, and does not have to hold all Confidential Information received from the disclosing party in trust and strict confidence, unless expressly directed by the disclosing party in writing. The receiving party is also not obligated to take any security precautions at all to keep confidential the disclosing party’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text places a strict obligation on the receiving party to protect and not disclose Confidential Information. The modified text completely removes these obligations, directly contradicting the core purpose of the NDA. This creates a direct contradiction with the original confidentiality obligations. This change directly contradicts the first sentence of the original text, creating a substantial conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the Tennessee Public Records Act, Section 10 -7-5-1 et seq., a t all times during the term of this Agreement and thereafter, the receiving party may disclose, directly or indirectly, any Confidential Information of the disclosing party to third parties, and does not have to hold all Confidential Information received from the disclosing party in trust and strict confidence, unless expressly directed by the disclosing party in writing. The receiving party is also not obligated to take any security precautions at all to keep confidential the disclosing party’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The Changed Text directly contradicts the Contradicted Text. The original text in the Changed Text imposes strict obligations to protect confidential information. The changed text removes these obligations, stating the receiving party 'may disclose' and 'does not have to hold all Confidential Information in trust' unless directed otherwise, and is 'not obligated to take any security precautions'. This is a direct contradiction and creates uncertainty within the document. The explanation and Perturbation Type 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' explain this too. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, proving it is an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the disclosing party’s request , all documents, materials and other items containing the disclosing party’s Confidential Information (including without limitation, all originals, copies, and reproductions of the Confidential Information) shall be promptly returned to the disclosing party without retaining a copy thereof, an d the receiving party shall provide a certification , signed by an officer, as to the completeness of the return of such material s. Upon such request, the receiving party also shall destroy all documents, materials and other items embodying or summarizing the disclosing party’s Confidential Information in wha tever format, and shall provide a similar certification as to the completeness of the destruction of such materials. Compliance with this Subsection 2(e) shall not relieve the receiving party of its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the disclosing party’s request, the receiving party may, at its sole discretion, return some or none of the documents, materials, and other items containing the disclosing party’s Confidential Information. The receiving party is under no obligation to provide any certification as to the completeness of any return. Further, the receiving party is not required to destroy any documents, materials, or other items containing or summarizing the disclosing party’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the return or destruction of Confidential Information upon request. The modified text makes the return optional and removes the obligation to destroy or certify. This creates an in-text contradiction with the original mandatory obligations outlined in the original text. This also allows the receiving party to retain materials thus undercutting confidentiality. The original text makes it an obligation, the modified text makes it at the discretion of the receiving party, making the return of confidential material optional.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the disclosing party’s request, the receiving party may, at its sole discretion, return some or none of the documents, materials, and other items containing the disclosing party’s Confidential Information. The receiving party is under no obligation to provide any certification as to the completeness of any return. Further, the receiving party is not required to destroy any documents, materials, or other items containing or summarizing the disclosing party’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intention of confidentiality outlined in the original text, which requires the receiving party to return or destroy confidential information. The modified text makes these actions optional, and it also removes the obligation to certify compliance. This change creates a clear conflict with the contradicted text, as the contradicted text is the changed text and directly makes the return of confidential material at the discretion of the receiving party. Thus it does not support the original text which stated an obligation.

perturbation.original_text: Each party acknowledge s that the other party’s Confidential Information is highly valuable, confidential and proprietary to the other party and has been developed through the investment of significant time, effort and expense, and that maintaining the confidentiality of this Confidential Information is essential for the general successful operations of TTU and Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party acknowledges that the other party’s Confidential Information has no value, is not confidential, and does not have any proprietary interest; developed without significant time, effort, and expense, and is not essential for the successful operations of TTU and Company.
perturbation.explanation: The original text emphasizes the high value and importance of Confidential Information. The modified text claims the opposite: that Confidential Information has no value and is not essential. This creates a fundamental contradiction, as it negates the very basis for the confidentiality agreement. This undermines the reason for the confidentiality agreement, in the first place. This change creates an in-text contradiction of the perceived value of the confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party acknowledges that the other party’s Confidential Information has no value, is not confidential, and does not have any proprietary interest; developed without significant time, effort, and expense, and is not essential for the successful operations of TTU and Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the confidential information has no value, is not confidential, and is not essential for the success of TTU and Company. This directly contradicts the definition of confidential information as valuable and essential, thus creating an in-text contradiction, according to the perturbation explanation. The contradicted text is a copy of the changed text, meaning that the contradiction is in the same location as the changed text.
